title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

title: A malicious actor with access to the network and low privileges could exploit a Path Traversal...
description: A malicious actor with access to the network and low privileges could exploit a Path Traversal vulnerability found in UniFi OS devices to access files on the underlying system that could be manipulated to obtain sensitive information.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Exploitation of the Path Traversal vulnerability in UniFi OS devices could allow attackers to access sensitive files on the system, potentially leading to data breaches, exposure of credentials, or further compromise of the network.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patches and firmware updates provided by the vendor to address CVE-2026-34911.
Restrict network access to UniFi OS devices by implementing strict firewall rules and network segmentation.
Monitor system logs for unusual file access patterns and unauthorized activities, and enable alerts for potential exploitation attempts.

title: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in SketchUp 2026's Dynamic Components feature allows...
description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in SketchUp 2026's Dynamic Components feature allows remote code execution and local file exfiltration through maliciously crafted SKP files. The vulnerability stems from improper input sanitization in the component options window, enabling attackers to execute arbitrary system commands and read local files without user interaction by exploiting an embedded Internet Explorer 11 browser.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The XSS vulnerability in SketchUp 2026's Dynamic Components feature could allow attackers to execute arbitrary system commands and exfiltrate local files, potentially leading to data breaches, unauthorized access, and system compromise. The use of an embedded Internet Explorer 11 browser exacerbates the risk due to its outdated security mechanisms.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply any available patches or updates provided by the vendor to address CVE-2026-9264.
Disable or restrict the use of the Dynamic Components feature until a fix is implemented, especially in environments handling sensitive data.
Implement strict file validation and scanning for SKP files before opening them, and educate users on the risks of opening files from untrusted sources.

title: A malicious actor with access to the network could exploit an Improper Access Control...
description: A malicious actor with access to the network could exploit an Improper Access Control vulnerability found in UniFi OS devices to make unauthorized changes to the system.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Exploitation of the Improper Access Control vulnerability in UniFi OS devices could allow malicious actors to make unauthorized changes to critical system configurations, potentially leading to service disruptions, data breaches, or further exploitation within the network.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the latest security patches provided by UniFi to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict network access to UniFi OS devices by implementing network segmentation and access controls.
Monitor system logs for unusual activity and conduct a thorough security audit of UniFi OS devices.

title: Concrete CMS 9 before 9.5.0 is vulnerable to Cross Site Request Forgery (CSRF) at concrete...
description: Concrete CMS 9 before 9.5.0 is vulnerable to Cross Site Request Forgery (CSRF) at concrete/controllers/backend/file rescan(). The Concrete CMS security team gave this vulnerability a CVSS v.4.0 score of 2.3 with vector CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:P/PR:N/UI:P/VC:N/VI:L/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N. Thanks Yonatan Drori (Tenzai) for reporting.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to perform Cross Site Request Forgery (CSRF) attacks, which could potentially trick authenticated users into executing unwanted actions on the server. However, the overall impact is limited due to the low CVSS score and specific attack vector requirements.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to Concrete CMS version 9.5.0 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement CSRF protection mechanisms, such as requiring CSRF tokens for sensitive operations.
Educate users and administrators about the risks of CSRF attacks and encourage safe browsing practices.

title: Concrete CMS 9.5.0 and below is vulnerable to IDOR in surveys. To be vulnerable, a site would...
description: Concrete CMS 9.5.0 and below is vulnerable to IDOR in surveys. To be vulnerable, a site would have to be configured in such a way that both public and private surveys are present on the site. An unauthenticated attacker can vote in the restricted survey by submitting the restricted optionID through the public survey’s endpoint. The Concrete CMS security team gave this vulnerability a CVSS v.4.0 score of 6.3 with vector CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:P/PR:N/UI:N/VC:N/VI:L/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N. Thanks  Zer0

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to exploit an IDOR flaw in Concrete CMS surveys, enabling them to submit votes to restricted surveys. This could lead to unauthorized data manipulation, skewed survey results, and potential reputational damage for organizations relying on survey integrity.
recommended_actions: Update Concrete CMS to the latest version above 9.5.0 to patch the vulnerability.
Audit survey configurations to ensure restricted surveys are not exposed to public endpoints.
Implement additional access controls and input validation to prevent unauthorized access to restricted resources.

title: Concrete CMS 9.5.0 and below is vulnerable to Reflected XSS in Legacy Pagination via HTML...
description: Concrete CMS 9.5.0 and below is vulnerable to Reflected XSS in Legacy Pagination via HTML attribute injection. Concrete\Core\Legacy\Pagination builds pagination links by raw-interpolating its $URL field into href="" (<a href="{$linkURL}" …>). Any authenticated admin or report viewer with access to `/dashboard/reports/forms/legacy` who clicks the crafted URL fires the payload in their session. The Concrete CMS security team gave this vulnerability a CVSS v.4.0 score of 6.0 with vector CVSS:4.0/AV

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8245.

title: CVE-2023-31317 - Improper restriction of operations within the bounds of a memory buffer in the AMD secure processer
description: Improper restriction of operations within the bounds of a memory buffer in the AMD secure processer (ASP) could allow an attacker to read or write to protected memory potentially resulting in arbitrary code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2023-31317.

title: CVE-2023-31316 - Improperly preserved integrity of hardware configuration state during a power save/restore operation
description: Improperly preserved integrity of hardware configuration state during a power save/restore operation in the AMD Secure Processor (ASP) could allow an attacker with the ability to write outside the trusted memory range (TMR) to change the execution flow of the Video Core Next (VCN) firmware potentially impacting confidentiality, integrity, or availability.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to exploit improperly preserved hardware configuration states during power save/restore operations, potentially altering the execution flow of firmware. This could lead to unauthorized access, data corruption, or denial of service, impacting system confidentiality, integrity, and availability.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest firmware and software updates provided by AMD to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement strict access controls to limit the ability of attackers to write outside the trusted memory range (TMR).
Monitor system logs and firmware behavior for any unusual activity that could indicate exploitation attempts.

title: CVE-2023-31309 - Improper validation in Power Management Firmware (PMFW) may allow an attacker with privileges to pas
description: Improper validation in Power Management Firmware (PMFW) may allow an attacker with privileges to pass malformed workload arguments when exporting table data from SMU to DRAM potentially resulting in a loss of confidentiality and/or availability.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Power Management Firmware (PMFW) could allow privileged attackers to exploit improper validation, potentially leading to a loss of confidentiality and availability. This may impact systems relying on PMFW for secure operations.
recommended_actions: Apply firmware updates or patches provided by the vendor to address CVE-2023-31309.
Restrict access to privileged accounts and monitor for unusual activity to minimize exploitation risk.
Implement additional validation checks for workload arguments in PMFW to prevent malformed data from being processed.

title: CVE-2026-8612 - WWW::Mechanize::Cached versions before 2.00 for Perl deserialize cached HTTP responses from a world-
description:
WWW::Mechanize::Cached versions before 2.00 for Perl deserialize cached HTTP responses from a world-writable on-disk cache, enabling local response forgery and code execution.

With no explicit cache backend, WWW::Mechanize::Cached constructs a default Cache::FileCache under /tmp/FileCache without overriding the backend's documented directory_umask of 000, so the cache root and its subdirectories are created mode 0777 with no sticky bit. Cache entries are named by sha1_hex of the request and read back through Storable::thaw on the next cache hit.

A local attacker with write access to the cache tree can replace a victim's cache entry for a known URL with an arbitrary frozen HTTP::Response blob, causing the victim's next get() of that URL to return attacker controlled response bytes. Because the bytes are passed to Storable::thaw, a victim process that has loaded any class with a side-effectful STORABLE_thaw, DESTROY, or overload hook can be escalated to arbitrary code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8612.

title: CVE-2026-0438 - A System Management Mode (SMM) handler could perform a callout to code located in non-SMM/untrusted
description: A System Management Mode (SMM) handler could perform a callout to code located in non-SMM/untrusted memory. A highly privileged attacker could, with active user interaction and under high complexity and present preconditions, trigger execution of attacker-controlled code in SMM, potentially compromising the system’s confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-0438.
